# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with this codebase.

## Documentation

- [docs/architecture.md](docs/architecture.md) - DDD modules, DI patterns, project structure
- [docs/code-style.md](docs/code-style.md) - Naming conventions, imports
- [docs/styling/overview.md](docs/styling/overview.md) - Theme system, @grapp/stacks, typography
- [docs/commands.md](docs/commands.md) - Dev and build commands

## Key Principles

### Framework-Agnostic Logic

Keep business logic in use cases, not in React components. UI should be a thin layer.

### Module Isolation

Each module has its own errors, types, events. Never use shared/global error types across modules.

### DI Patterns

```typescript
// Symbol tokens for replaceable dependencies
export const FEATURE_REPOSITORY = Symbol("FEATURE_REPOSITORY");
container.register(FEATURE_REPOSITORY, FeatureRepository);

// Direct class injection for concrete implementations
constructor(
  @inject(FEATURE_REPOSITORY) private readonly repo: FeatureRepository,
) {}
```

## Skill Overrides

Skills under `.agents/skills/` come from `expo/skills` (see `skills-lock.json`) and describe generic Expo conventions. When they conflict with this project, **project conventions win**.

### File naming (overrides `building-native-ui`)

- Components / screens / HOCs → **PascalCase** (`PositionCard.tsx`, `WalletsScreen.tsx`, `withAuth.tsx`)
- Hooks → **camelCase** matching the export (`usePositionsQuery.ts`)
- Domain / infrastructure → **kebab-case + dotted suffix** (`positions.repository.ts`, `delete-wallet.usecase.ts`, `position.events.ts`, `wallets.store.ts`, `positions.keys.ts`)
- Special files keep Expo Router conventions (`_layout.tsx`, `+not-found.tsx`)
- Full table: [docs/code-style.md](docs/code-style.md)

### Routes location (overrides `building-native-ui`)

- Routes live in **`src/app/`**, not `app/`. Configured via the `expo-router` plugin in `app.json` (`"root": "./src/app"`).

### Styling (overrides `building-native-ui`)

- Always use **`react-native-unistyles` `StyleSheet.create((theme) => ...)`**. **Never** inline styles, even though the `building-native-ui` skill recommends them.
- Layout composition uses **`@grapp/stacks`** (`Stack`, `Inline`, `Box`, `Columns`) — prefer these over hand-rolled `flexDirection` + `gap`.
- Theme tokens come from **`@mars-909/theme`** (Material Design 3, 5 variants × light/dark). Don't reach for raw colors.

### Storage (overrides `native-data-fetching`)

- General persistence is **`react-native-mmkv`** (+ `mobx-persist-store` for MobX state). Use it for app state, caches, preferences, non-sensitive data.
- For genuinely sensitive secrets (wallet private keys, refresh tokens, API credentials) **`expo-secure-store`** is the right tool — it's backed by iOS Keychain / Android Keystore. Not currently in the dependency tree; introduce it when you actually need it, don't shoehorn secrets into mmkv.
- Do **not** introduce `AsyncStorage` — superseded by mmkv project-wide.

### Data fetching (overrides `native-data-fetching`)

- HTTP client is **`openapi-fetch`** against types generated by `bun run codegen` from `apps/server/openapi/*.json` (output: `src/core/api-client/generated/gateway.d.ts`, `src/features/uniswap-v3/data/generated/uniswap-v3.d.ts`).
- Do **not** call raw `fetch` for backend endpoints; use the typed client.
- React Query keys are organized via **`@lukemorales/query-key-factory`** (see `*.keys.ts` files).
- For on-chain reads use **`viem`** — not raw RPC fetches.

### API routes (replaces `expo-api-routes`)

- The mobile app does **not** host API routes (`+api.ts`). Backend lives in the separate **`apps/server`** workspace and exposes OpenAPI to mobile via codegen. The `expo-api-routes` skill has been removed from this project; do not introduce server-side endpoints into `src/app/`.

### Architecture (not covered by any skill)

- DDD feature modules at `src/<feature>/` with `di/`, `domain/`, `data/`, `application/`, `presentation/` layers.
- DI via **`tsyringe`**: Symbol tokens for repositories/services (replaceable), direct class injection for use cases/event handlers.
- Business logic lives in use cases. UI components stay thin.
- Path aliases: `core/*`, `positions/*`, `wallets/*`, `onboarding/*`, `types/*` — prefer over relative imports across module boundaries.
- See [docs/architecture.md](docs/architecture.md).
